title: IPv6 and Modern Programmable Networks
description: Extend the branch design for IPv6, centralized policy, overlays, zero trust, SASE, and infrastructure as code. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a dual-stack and programmable-network transition plan while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use IPv6 notation, types, scopes, neighbor behavior, and transition choices.
Explain SDN, SD-WAN, VXLAN, ZTA, SASE, and SSE without marketing shortcuts.
Treat network automation as reviewed software with drift and rollback controls.
